questions.prompt: Binary search on a sorted array of length n
questions.explanation: Each comparison throws away half the remaining elements: n → n/2 → n/4 → … You can only halve n about log₂n times before one element is left. Halving the problem each step is the signature of logarithmic time.
questions.cardBack: Each step halves the search space → log₂n steps → O(log n)

questions.explanation: A hash function maps the key straight to a slot index, so lookup jumps directly there with no scanning — constant time on average. (Worst case is O(n) if many keys collide into one spot.)
questions.visual:
key ──hash()──▶ slot 3 ──▶ value
        (jump straight there, no scan)
questions.cardBack: hash(key) → slot index directly, no scan → O(1) average

questions.explanation: Splitting in half repeatedly creates log n levels. Merging the pieces at each level touches all n elements once. log n levels × n work per level = O(n log n).
questions.cardBack: split in half → log n levels; merge → n per level → O(n log n)

questions.prompt: Generating all permutations of n items
questions.explanation: A permutation is an ordering. There are n choices for the first slot, n−1 for the second, n−2 for the third, … down to 1: n × (n−1) × … × 1 = n!.
questions.visual:
slot:  1st    2nd    3rd
        n  ×  n-1  × n-2 … × 1  = n!
3 items → 3! = 6 orderings

questions.prompt: Generating all subsets of n items
questions.explanation: Build a subset by deciding IN or OUT for each item — n independent yes/no choices, 2 options each: 2 × 2 × … × 2 = 2ⁿ. (Contrast permutations, which arrange in order → n!.)
questions.visual:
each item → IN or OUT?  (2 choices)
  a     b     c
  2  ×  2  ×  2  = 2³ = 8

questions.prompt: Single pass over an n-element list
questions.explanation: One loop visits each of the n elements exactly once, doing constant work each time: n × O(1) = O(n).
questions.visual:
[• • • • • • • •]
 →→→→→→→→  visit each once
 n elements → O(n)
questions.cardBack: visit each of n elements once → O(n)

questions.prompt: Inserting at index 0 of a Python list of length n
questions.explanation: A Python list is a contiguous array. Inserting at the front means every existing element must shift one slot to the right to make room — n shifts. (Appending to the end is O(1) amortized.)
questions.visual:
insert x at 0:
[a b c d]
 ↓ shift all right
[x a b c d]
 n elements moved → O(n)
questions.cardBack: front insert shifts all n elements right → O(n)

questions.prompt: Building a string with += over n single-char iterations
questions.explanation: Strings are immutable, so each += builds a brand-new string and copies every character so far. Copying 1 + 2 + … + n characters totals n(n+1)/2 = O(n²). Collect into a list and "".join() at the end for O(n).
questions.visual:
+= copies the string-so-far each time:
 1 + 2 + 3 + … + n = n(n+1)/2 → O(n²)
fix: list.append + "".join → O(n)
questions.cardBack: immutable string → each += copies all chars; 1+2+…+n = O(n²). Use "".join → O(n)
